factors.name: Price Action - Momentum Loss
factors.summary: NAS100 down -2.73% in 24h and -1.47% in 4h, signaling sustained selling pressure after record highs earlier in the week. Move is in progress but not yet deeply extended.

factors.name: Fed Hawkishness & Real Yields
factors.summary: Fed Governor Barr warned of risks from deregulation proposals, criticizing moves to 'considerably weaken bank regulation.' ECB stepping up as 'lead hawk' with rate hike primed—spillover hawkishness pressures long-duration growth assets.

factors.name: June Swoon / Seasonal Weakness
factors.summary: MarketWatch article explicitly warns 'Don't rule out a June swoon — the S&P 500 is pushing its limits.' Market stretched after strong earnings but sentiment turning cautious as rally runs into seasonal headwinds.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk - Iran War Escalation
factors.summary: War in Iran approaches 100-day mark with ongoing missile/drone exchanges. Container shipping rates up 109% since war started; stagflation narrative emerging via IATA commentary on rising jet fuel costs and rate hike risk.

factors.name: Tech Valuation Stretch & Derating Risk
factors.summary: Macro analysts and credit managers (DoubleLine, Oaktree) bracing for 'potential AI pain' and are buying defensive debt. Robo-advisors cannot profit from AI stock picks; earnings beat sentiment positive but valuations already priced in.

factors.name: Crypto Contagion & Risk-Off Correlation
factors.summary: Bitcoin worst weekly rout since FTX, down >50% from Trump-reelection highs; cryptos shed $390B. NQ-BTC correlation historically shows tech weakness when crypto crashes—heightened risk-off spillover into NDX.

factors.name: South Korea Overheating & Global Equity Caution
factors.summary: Investors hedging SK stock positions as market reaches 'hottest' valuations; Samsung/SK Hynix rate-hike sensitivity could trigger 15% correction. Signals broader EM/developed market caution and semiconductor appetite cooling.

factors.name: Earnings Season Strength (Residual Bullish Offset)
factors.summary: Strongest earnings season in years with Wall Street analysts rarely this bullish on individual names. However, this tailwind is being overwhelmed by macro headwinds and sentiment shifts.

factors.name: Upcoming Major Catalysts - Oracle Earnings & AI Narrative Risk
factors.summary: Oracle earnings next week could stabilize sentiment or accelerate selloff if guidance disappoints. AI capex narrative pivotal but no new bullish catalyst visible in news window.

factors.name: Semiconductor Supply Chain - Mixed Signals
factors.summary: Boeing securing 200-jet China order suggests capex resilience, but TSMC/Samsung export control tensions and SK overheating suggest incremental demand caution. No major supply shock but momentum broken.

screening.narrative: NAS100 has rolled over sharply after testing record highs earlier in the week, now down -2.73% in 24h as a confluence of macro headwinds (ECB hawkishness, Iran war stagflation, real yield re-pricing) and equity valuation caution overwhelm late-cycle earnings strength. Crypto collapse (-$390B in a week) and global risk-off sentiment in South Korea suggest growing unease about stretched growth multiples at a moment when Fed tightening is re-accelerating overseas. The move is in Phase 2 (underway but not yet exhausted), with June seasonal weakness narrative ('June swoon') gaining traction alongside earnings euphoria wearing off.
catalysts_ahead: Oracle earnings (early next week) — major AI capex narrative pivot point
ECB rate decision (imminent per news) — hawkishness spillover to USD/Treasury yields
CPI/PCE release (likely next 48-72h) — inflation surprise could re-price Fed terminal rate
FOMC meeting (mid-June window likely) — forward guidance on rate cuts/hikes critical
NVDA earnings / hyperscaler cloud capex commentary — semiconductor/AI demand barometer
China tech regulation or EV tariff escalation — NDX international revenue headwind
screening.risk_warning: Move is already in progress (-2.73% 24h); if Oracle/MSFT next-week earnings crush expectations and reaffirm cloud capex, sentiment could snap back and invalidate the SELL bias. Oversold technical setup (VIX now catching up per news) could trigger relief rally. Additionally, if Fed signals dovish pause or inflation data cools unexpectedly, real yields collapse and growth multiples re-expand, negating the hawkish macro narrative.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 — avoid chasing shorts here. Wait for retest of 29,200-29,300 resistance or a failed pop above 29,000 mid-week before re-shorting. For longs: wait for capitulation signal or oversold bounce setup (RSI <30) before covering shorts and buying dips toward 28,600 support.

factors.name: Price action momentum
factors.summary: 4h down -1.47%, 24h down -2.73% — multi-hour sell-off already underway. Move is extended into Phase 3; late-stage chasing risky.

factors.name: ECB rate hike primed (this week)
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports ECB stepping up as 'lead hawk' with rate hike coming imminently. Rising real yields globally compress long-duration Nasdaq multiples.

factors.name: Real yields pressure
factors.summary: ECB tightening cycle + global stagflation signals (Iran war 100 days in, oil cost pass-through) push real yields higher, structurally bearish for mega-cap growth/AI stocks.

factors.name: Crypto correlation breakdown
factors.summary: Bitcoin cratering (worst weekly rout since FTX, $390B crypto shed this week per CoinDesk). BTC-NQ linkage suggests risk-off sentiment; equity volatility likely to persist.

factors.name: Earnings/forward guidance gap
factors.summary: Strong earnings season closing, but Wall Street macro soothsayers and equity analysts diverge sharply on forward outlook. Hyperscaler capex commentary next week critical (Oracle earnings highlighted).

factors.name: June swoon warning
factors.summary: MarketWatch explicitly warns of 'June swoon' risk; S&P 500 'pushing its limits.' Record highs followed by avalanche selling on Friday (this week). Overbought unwind in progress.

factors.name: South Korea hedge surge
factors.summary: MarketWatch reports Korean bull market 'overheating' — investors hedging, paring crowded trades; rate hike threatens 15% correction. Tech supply chain (Samsung, SK Hynix) are NQ components.

factors.name: Fed deregulation risk
factors.summary: Fed Governor Barr warns looser Wall Street bank rules 'considerably weaken' regulation in a financial boom. Macro policy uncertainty and potential systemic stress weigh on sentiment.

factors.name: Mega-cap earnings near-term uncertainty
factors.summary: No major FAANG earnings in the immediate 24-72h window. Oracle next week will be key. Lack of near-term catalysts limits upside catalyst conviction.

factors.name: Dollar direction mixed
factors.summary: No major DXY move cited in news. ECB hiking may briefly support EUR, but USD strength from escalating geopolitical risk (Iran war, Israel-Lebanon) could offset. Neutral on NQ FX hedge.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is in the midst of a Phase 3 extended sell-off (-2.73% in 24h) following a record-high rally earlier in the week. The primary driver is a confluence of hawkish macro headwinds: ECB rate hike imminent this week, global real yields rising, and crypto contagion (BTC down sharply, risk-off tone). South Korean equity overheating and hedge flows suggest crowded longs are unwinding. While earnings season has been strong, forward guidance gaps and macro policy uncertainty (Fed deregulation debate, Iran war stagflation) are compressing growth multiples. June swoon narrative is now live.
catalysts_ahead: ECB rate decision (this week — ~June 12) → hawkish surprise = further NQ compression
Oracle earnings (next week) + cloud/AI capex guidance → critical for hyperscaler demand narrative
US CPI/Core CPI data (mid-June) → inflation surprise would re-price Fed terminal rate and compress multiples
FOMC minutes (if released) → any hawkish hints = additional selling
NVDA, MSFT, AAPL earnings (dates TBD, likely late June) → mega-cap capex and margin commentary
China-Taiwan or geopolitical escalation → semiconductor supply chain shocks
Bitcoin/crypto stabilization (or further dump) → risk sentiment barometer for equities
screening.risk_warning: Move is extended; if NQ rebounds decisively through 29,200 on Monday with positive hyperscaler news or weaker-than-expected inflation print, SELL bias invalidates. Conversely, if ECB hike is dovish-er than priced or geopolitical tensions ease abruptly, support could be tested. Watch for capitulation volume on further drops — that could trigger mean reversion rally into 29,100–29,300.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is already extended 2.73% down and into Phase 3 — wait for retest of 28,800–29,000 support zone before considering fresh short. If support breaks (28,600), next leg down likely 28,200–28,000. For longs, wait for confirmed reversal pattern + close above 29,300 with volume before re-engaging.
